title: [lichen_l2.c:556-588] Double signature verification on constant-time iteration
description:
What: peer_try_all_pubkeys() attempts constant-time iteration for security, but on finding a match it saves the index and continues. If a SECOND peer also matches, it overwrites found_idx only if found_idx<0 (line 556). However, the final code at lines 582-586 uses peer_table[found_idx], not the original matched values - which is correct.

Why it matters: This is actually NOT a bug. On re-read, the code correctly:
1. Records only the FIRST match (if found_idx < 0)
2. Continues checking remaining peers (constant time)
3. Uses the first matched peer's values at the end

The code is correct. Closing this as not-a-bug.

Evidence: Line 556 'if (found_idx < 0)' ensures only first match is recorded.
close_reason: Not a bug - code analysis confirms the constant-time iteration is implemented correctly with if (found_idx < 0) guard.
